name: Penne Alla Puttane D'elba
recipeIngredient: 1/2 pound young zucchini, sliced paper thin
12 large leaves basil, torn in half
1 pound overripe tomatoes, roughly chopped, retaining seeds and liquid
2 tablespoons giant capers, rinsed of salt, drained
1 pound dried penne pasta, preferably Italian
recipeInstructions:
1. In a large bowl, combine the zucchini, garlic, basil, tomatoes (with seeds and liquid), olive oil, capers, olives, lemon juice, crushed red pepper flakes, and salt.
2. Allow the mixture to stand in a cool place for 30 minutes.
3. Meanwhile, bring 6 quarts of water to a boil in a spaghetti pot. Add 2 tablespoons of salt.
4. Cook the penne pasta according to package instructions until al dente, about 10 to 12 minutes.
5. Drain the pasta and add it to the bowl with the vegetables.
6. Stir gently to mix well and serve immediately.
This dish is not served hot.
